items.title: UConn Coach Dan Hurley Brings His Wannabe Bob Knight Imitation To Indiana
items.summary: Dan Hurley is explosive as a college basketball coach. He also is successful. Not that folks have to like either one, and they don't. Not unless they cheer for UConn.

items.title: Dipping Into ‘Rejection Therapy’ As A Self-Behavioral Resiliency Approach Via AI Guidance
items.summary: People are trying out rejection therapy. It is informal. Best to use AI as an advisor to get things right. I explain how. An AI Insider scoop.

items.title: ‘No one is saying, ‘I want more cancer with my candy”: Why Peeps are a ‘food chemical success story’ despite RFK Jr’s campaign to destroy their dyes
items.summary: Peeps, that holiday candy tradition of bunnies and chicks that get eaten or are left to stale, are at the center of a food dye battle with MAHA.

items.title: Meet the Gen Z grads reviving accounting—colleges are reporting near-perfect placement rates at $80K starting salaries
items.summary: Once seen as boring and synonymous with tax season dread, accounting is now attracting Gen Z with high demand, near six-figure salaries, and near-guaranteed job offers.

items.title: Chasing trends or buying value? The strategy that wins over time
items.summary: The global stock market landscape has become increasingly complex, shaped by macroeconomic uncertainty, geopolitical risks, and shifting liquidity conditions. Yet, amid this volatility, timeless investing principles, such as those advocated by Joel Greenblatt offer a structured lens to interpret market behavior and identify opportunities.

items.title: I’m a VC who bets on AI. What keeps me up at night isn’t the idea of these companies failing—quite the opposite
items.summary: I've stopped asking, "which AI company should I back?" Instead, I've started asking which problems the major players are ignoring.

items.title: 'Anything but autos': Can defense save Europe’s ailing car industry?
